rulings.Case Title: JASON VISCARRA VS. ELLEN POON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment And/Or Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Jdugment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 473(B) And 1008(A)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2026. Line 4.2. PLAINTIFF JASON VISCARRA Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment And/Or Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Jdugment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 473(B) And 1008(A) is continued to July 23, 2026. This action was filed on June 10, 2024. Plaintiff was represented by Sellar, Hazard & Lucia since the inception of this action. Defendant appeared in this action on July 25, 2024. The trial was set for December 8, 2025 on April 11, 2025. On April 29, 2025 Defendant filed a motion for summary judgment setting it for July 25, 2025 hearing. Plaintiff filed an opposition to the motion for summary judgment accompanied by a separate statement and a Declaration of Justin Graham on July 2, 2026. No other evidence was submitted with the opposition. Neither the opposition brief nor the Declaration of Justin Graham requested a continuance of the motion. CCP 437c(h) is not mentioned (See Table of Authorities) and its requirements are not met (See Declaration of Justin Graham). The Court record does not reflect any independent requests for continuance of the Motion for Summary Judgment hearing for discovery (e.g. ex-parte or motion) before the motion hearing on October 30, 2025 in department 501. Motion for Summary Judgment was granted, but no order has been entered. On November 25, 2026 Plaintiff filed an Ex-parte Application for an order setting a hearing on a Motion for Relief From Judgment/Reconsideration in department 302. This filing does not appear to include any explanation for setting a hearing to vacate a ruling issued by department 501 in department 302. Ex-parte application was not addressed on the merits by department 302 and the parties were instructed that this matter is to be presented in department 501. On December 1, 2025 Plaintiff re-filed the similarly titled ex-parte application for department 501. Ex-parte application was not granted. Order Granting Summary Judgment was entered on December 24, 2025. Motion for Relief from Judgement and/or Reconsideration of Order Granting Summary Judgment was filed on December 31, 2025. The Court requested supplemental briefing in conjunction with this motion due to relevant information missing in the motion. According to the Notice of Motion it is based on "newly-discovered evidence and controlling legal authority" and "due to inadvertence of behalf of Plaintiff; s former attorney) and "this attorney's failure to attach Expert Witness Testimony attributing causation to Defendant's failure to inspect visually obvious corrosion and frayed supply lines." Presumably "this attorney" is Mr. Todd Knudsen, who signed the Notice of Motion. Since the relief under CCP 473(b) is sought in conjunction with the Order Granting Summary Judgment, presumably the Expert Witness Testimony was to be (but was not) attached to a filing in conjunction with said motion (which would have been Plaintiff's opposition), but Mr. Knudsen's involvement with that filing is not explained. Additionally, according to the supplemental declaration of Megan Alexander filed on April 1, 2026, expert findings have been provided on November 5, 2025, months after Opposition to the Motion for Summary -- continued

rulings.Case Title: JASON VISCARRA VS. ELLEN POON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: CONTINUED TENTATIVE RULING FOR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM JUDGMENT OR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: CONTINUED TENTATIVE RULING FOR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM JUDGMENT OR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT ---Judgment was filed on July 5, 2025 (and it would not have been possible for counsel to have "failed" to attach something that did not yet exist to the filing). Ms. Alexander's declaration (Section V) proceeds to state that the purported omissions pertained to filings in November 2025. It is not clear how omissions relating to filings in November 2025 are relevant to relief sought in conjunction with the motion for summary judgment has have been fully briefed, heard and ruled upon months before November 2025. Therefore, it is unclear from the Notice of Motion exactly what relief is being sought and why under CCP 473(b) as it relates to the Order Granting for Summary Judgment when inadvertent omissions were in conjunction with filing of a motion for reconsideration in November 2025. Points and Authorities in support of the Motion discuss the relief under CCP 473(b) in conjunction with retained expert testimony "due to attorney error" making statements such as "attorney error leads to exclusions of significant evidence" and "failure to consider such evidence materially affected the ruling and warrants discretionary relief." According to the supplemental declaration of Ms. Alexander, expert has not been retained and his "testimony" has not been available until after the hearing on the Motion for Summary Judgment. It is again unclear how evidence that did not exist could have been "excluded" or could have been subject to "failure to consider." Neither the notice of motion, points and authorities nor the supplemental declaration of Ms. Alexander shed any light on the request for relief under CCP 473(b) as it pertains to the ruling on the Motion for Summary Judgment at the time that it has been made. It is possible that the moving party is seeking relief for its failure to obtain the evidence earlier or to time seeks continuance for discovery, however, the Court would not venture to guess, especially on a motion based on attorney error, which has to be documented in a declaration per CCP 473(b). As to the "newly-discovered" evidence in support of the motion for reconsideration, there is no explanation whatsoever why this evidence has not been obtained earlier given the procedural posture and the timeline of this case. For example, why experts have not been retained earlier? Why defendant has not been deposed earlier/if there were issues with deposition why no motion to compel or a motion to continue hearing on the motion for summary judgment was not filed? The motion (including all supplemental filings) is confusing, incomprehensible and not in compliance with procedural requirements. This appears to be a pattern. See ex-parte application filed in a wrong department, opposition separate statement not in conformity with the rules, omitted declarations, notices of errata, etc. The Court is to rule on the cases on the merits. It is impossible to determine the merits of this motion as it is drafted and presented to the Court at this time. The Court intends to avoid an additional CCP 473(b) motion based on failure to properly present this CCP 1008/473(b) motion. Therefore, the Court will continue the hearing on this motion to July 23, 2026. No later than June 25, 2026 Plaintiff to file a coherent, well-organized and properly supported motion (including notice of motion, motion, points and authorities and evidence); opposition and reply to be filed in accordance with the new hearing date. =(501/CFH)
